response:
This 74-year-old female with a history of HTN, hyperlipidemia, and CAD was admitted for surgical management of symptomatic severe aortic stenosis (aortic valve area 0.5 cm²). 
 She underwent an elective CABG x2 (Vein to Obtuse Marginal and Posterior Left Ventricular Branch), aortic valve replacement (21mm porcine bioprosthesis), and PFO closure. 
 Preoperative echocardiography confirmed severe AS and a patent foramen ovale with a left-to-right shunt. 
 Post-bypass imaging demonstrated a well-seated valve with no paravalvular leak and an intact atrial septum. 
 Her postoperative hospital course was notable for new-onset atrial fibrillation (managed with amiodarone and warfarin), postoperative anemia requiring a two-unit PRBC transfusion, and transient confusion with leukocytosis (WBC 28.1), for which she was pancultured and started on Ciprofloxacin. 
 Radiographic studies showed expected post-surgical atelectasis and small bilateral pleural effusions without pneumothorax. 
 By postoperative day six, the patient’s mental status had returned to baseline, her INR was therapeutic at 3.6, and she was stable for discharge. 
 She was sent home with services on a medication regimen including Amiodarone, Warfarin, Metoprolol Tartrate, Aspirin, Atorvastatin, Furosemide, and a finishing course of Ciprofloxacin, with instructions to observe sternal precautions and monitor for signs of infection or fluid overload.
